cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
cider: noun. An alcoholic, often sparkling (carbonated) beverage made from fermented apples.
cinder: noun. Partially or mostly burnt material that results from incomplete combustion of coal or wood etc.; it often rides the rising smoke
credit: verb. To believe; to put credence in.
cretin: noun. A person who fails to develop mentally and physically due to a congenital hypothyroidism.
cried: verb. simple past and past participle of cry
crinite: adjective. Having the appearance of a tuft of hair.
den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
dice: noun. Gaming with one or more dice.
dicer: noun. A gambler who plays dice.
dicier: adjective. comparative form of dicey: more dicey
dineric: adjective. Of a liquid: eddying or whirling.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dire: adjective. Warning of bad consequences: ill-boding; portentous.
direct: adjective. Proceeding without deviation or interruption.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
dite: verb. To prepare for use or action; to make ready.
edict: noun. A proclamation of law or other authoritative command.
edit: noun. A change to the text of a document.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
icier: adjective. comparative form of icy: more icy
incited: verb. simple past and past participle of incite
indict: verb. To accuse of wrongdoing; charge.
indie: adjective. Independent; from outside the mainstream.
indirect: adjective. Not direct: Not of obvious or immediate cause, but as a secondary result.
indite: verb. To physically make (letters and words) on a writing surface; to inscribe.
indri: noun. One of the largest living lemurs (Indri indri), native to Madagascar.
inter: verb. To bury in a grave.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
ired: verb. simple past and past participle of ire
irenic: adjective. Promoting or fitted to promote peace or peacemaking, especially over disputes; conciliatory, non-confrontational, peaceful.
irid: noun. The iris of the eye.
nerd: noun. A person who is intellectual but generally introverted.
neritic: adjective. Of or relating to a marine environment of shallow waters.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nicer: adjective. comparative form of nice: more nice
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
niter: noun. A mineral form of potassium nitrate (saltpetre) used in making gunpowder.
nitre: noun. UK standard spelling of niter.
nitric: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or containing, nitrogen.
nitride: noun. A compound of nitrogen where nitrogen has an oxidation state of −3.
red: noun. The colour of the setting sun, blood, and strawberries; the colour which is evoked by the longest visible wavelengths (between about
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
rend: verb. To separate into parts with force or sudden violence; to split; to burst.
rent: noun. A payment made by a tenant at intervals in order to lease a property.
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
riced: adjective. Made into or consisting of finely minced pieces resembling rice
ricin: noun. An extremely toxic lectin extracted from the castor bean.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
ride: verb. To transport oneself by sitting on and directing a horse, later also a bicycle etc.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
teiid: adjective. Of or belonging to the Teiidae family of tropical American lizards.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tide: noun. The daily fluctuation in the level of the sea caused by the gravitational influence of the moon and the sun.
tire: verb. To become sleepy or weary.
tired: verb. simple past and past participle of tire
trend: noun. An inclination in a particular direction.
trice: verb. To pull, to pull out or away, to pull sharply.
triced: verb. simple past and past participle of trice
tried: adjective. Tested, hence, proven to be firm or reliable.
trined: verb. simple past and past participle of trine
